note: Scoped code-smell scan of a path/module/diff (read-only findings register). Discriminators: SCOPE (one path/module/diff, not the whole project → refactor-audit), READ-ONLY (produces a report, does not plan → refactor-plan, or mutate code → refactor-apply), and QUALITY-not-CORRECTNESS (smells/anti-patterns/naming, not bugs/security → pr-review/debug). mock_rate encodes the ideal outcome for --decider mock; real deciders ignore it.

queries.text: check src/api for code smells before I start refactoring it
scan the storage layer for anti-patterns and long functions
any smells in packages/marvin-mcp-shared/src/contracts? feels bloated
review the naming consistency across the tools/ dir, the verbs are all over the place (fetch vs get vs load)
is server.ts too big? give me a findings register with file:line evidence
my manager asked me to look for dead code and duplication in the flows module before we touch it
marvin smell check on the diff vs dev
scan the staged changes for god objects and copy-paste drift, dont touch anything
look over prompts/index.ts for anti-patterns and stringly-typed stuff, evidence only no fixes
give me a scoped code-health report on the kanban storage folder, primitive obsession and boolean flags especially
sequence F1 and F3 from the smell report into a refactoring plan with rollback steps
queries.note: findings already exist; turning them into a plan is refactor-plan

queries.text: apply F4 from the register — do the actual refactor step
review this PR diff for bugs and security issues
queries.note: correctness/security review → pr-review, not a quality smell scan

queries.text: explain how the verify tool's concurrent gate runner works
this function returns undefined sometimes, help me find the root cause
queries.note: a bug to root-cause → debug, not a smell scan

queries.text: plan the migration from the old four-server layout to the single server
run the linter and eslint --fix over src/
queries.note: plain shell/tooling command, not a reasoned findings register

queries.text: audit the whole codebase structure and tell me where the tech debt is
queries.note: whole-project structural audit → refactor-audit (scope is the whole repo, not a path)

queries.text: these dependencies look outdated, check them for known CVEs
reformat this file with prettier and sort the imports
queries.note: mechanical formatting, no analysis — plain tooling, not a smell scan

queries.text: do a code-health check on just the src/api module — long functions, duplication, that kind of thing
queries.note: 'code-health check' is refactor-audit vocab, but the scope is a single module → refactor-smells wins on scope

queries.text: scan the diff vs dev for smells and anti-patterns
queries.note: must beat pr-review — a diff scan for quality smells, not a correctness/security review

queries.text: refactoring audit of the whole repo — find the hotspots and dependency tangles first
queries.note: whole-project structural audit with hotspots → refactor-audit must win

queries.text: we've got the smell register — now plan how to pay this debt down in ordered steps
queries.note: register already produced; sequencing findings into a plan → refactor-plan must win
